Session Management.details: Creates a new terminal session that can be detached and reattached later.
Starts a new session with a specific label, making it easier to manage multiple sessions.
Displays all currently active screen sessions and their status (Attached or Detached).
Reconnects to a session that was previously detached.
Reconnects to a specific named session that was previously detached.

Session Management.desc: Detach a running session and reattach here
Session Management.details: Forcibly detaches a session from another terminal and reattaches it to the current one.
Disconnects the current terminal from the screen session while keeping the session running.
Detaches the session and also logs out the user from the remote server.
Exits the shell within the screen session; if it's the last window, the session terminates.

Windows.details: Opens a new virtual terminal window within the current screen session.
Moves the focus to the next window in the list.
Moves the focus to the previous window in the list.

Windows.desc: Switch to a window by number (0-9)
Windows.details: Directly jumps to the window with the corresponding index.

Windows.desc: Prompt for a window name or number to switch to
Windows.details: Opens a prompt where you can type the name or index of the window you want to switch to.
Allows you to change the title of the current window for easier identification.

Windows.desc: List all windows (displayed in the status bar)
Windows.details: Shows a list of all active windows in the bottom status line.
Opens a full-screen interactive menu to select a window from the list.
Closes the currently active window after a confirmation prompt.

Windows.desc: Kill all windows and terminate the session
Windows.details: Immediately terminates all windows and ends the entire screen session.

Regions (Splits).details: Divides the current display area into two horizontal regions.
Divides the current display area into two vertical regions.

Regions (Splits).desc: Switch input focus to the next region
Regions (Splits).details: Moves the cursor and focus to the next available region on the screen.
Removes the currently focused region from the display (does not kill the window).

Regions (Splits).desc: Close all regions except the current one
Regions (Splits).details: Removes all other regions, making the current region fill the entire screen.

Copy Mode.details: Allows you to scroll back through the terminal history and select text.

Copy Mode.desc: Start a selection in copy mode
Copy Mode.details: Begins marking text for copying from the current cursor position.
Copies the marked text into the screen buffer and exits copy mode.
Inserts the content of the screen buffer into the current window.
Moves the view one page up in copy/scrollback mode.
Moves the view one page down in copy/scrollback mode.

Miscellaneous.details: Displays a comprehensive list of screen commands and their associated key bindings.

Miscellaneous.desc: Toggle between current and previous windows
Miscellaneous.details: Quickly switches back and forth between the two most recently used windows.
Opens a prompt at the bottom of the screen for entering internal screen commands.

Miscellaneous.desc: Toggle logging the current window to a file
Miscellaneous.details: Starts or stops writing the output of the current window to a file named 'screenlog.n'.
